summary: The reproduction bundle clones the vulnerable upstream commit, runs the public chunk endpoint under a tiny tmpfs, and posts repeated unauthenticated chunk bodies until orphaned YTPChunk_* files fill temporary storage. The bundle was generated and ./reproduction/run.sh was executed, but it failed immediately on this host because docker is not installed.
expected_security_impact: Running the reproduction should fill the container tmpfs with orphaned YTPChunk_* files created by repeated unauthenticated POSTs to /objects/aVideoEncoderChunk.json.php, then emit EVOHUNT_CHUNK_TMPFS_FILLED once later requests fail under the exhausted tmp directory.
actual_observed_result: Executing ./reproduction/run.sh on this host exited with status 127 and stderr `docker is required to run this reproduction`, so the expected tmpfs-exhaustion proof signal could not be observed in-session.
verification_method: The helper starts PHP with sys_temp_dir pointed at a 10 MiB tmpfs, posts repeated 3 MiB bodies to the public chunk endpoint, records the orphaned YTPChunk_* files under /work/tmp, and treats tmpfs exhaustion plus a later failed request as the proof oracle.
evidence_produced_by_run: ./reproduction/run.sh was executed on the host and exited with status 127.
The host-side stderr was: docker is required to run this reproduction.
limitations_or_blockers: The current host environment does not provide the docker command required by the mandatory Docker-based reproduction flow.

reproduction.source_acquisition: run.sh clones the public WWBN/AVideo repository on the host, fetches the pinned vulnerable commit, checks it out detached, and mounts that checkout into the container at /target-repo.
